title: Intermediate Interview Question Bank
subtitle: Bộ quiz phỏng vấn trung cấp tập trung vào trade-off triển khai, debug, tối ưu, API, automation và persistence.

questions.question: Vì sao nên validation happen near the API boundary?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì các request không hợp lệ nên bị từ chối trước khi làm bẩn logic nghiệp vụ.
questions.explanation: Boundary validation simplifies downstream assumptions and produces clearer errors.
questions.question_en: Why should validation happen near the API boundary?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao nên validation happen near the API boundary?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because invalid requests should be rejected before they contaminate business logic.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Bởi vì các request không hợp lệ nên bị từ chối trước khi làm bẩn logic nghiệp vụ.
questions.explanation_en: Boundary validation simplifies downstream assumptions and produces clearer errors.
questions.explanation_vi: Boundary validation simplifies downstream assumptions and produces clearer errors.

questions.question: Vì sao a timeout plus retry strategy often better than no timeout for external API calls?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì it bounds waiting time and provides a path for tạm thời failure recovery.
questions.explanation: Nếu không có các timeout one slow dependency can stall the whole workflow.
questions.question_en: Why is a timeout plus retry strategy often better than no timeout for external API calls?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao a timeout plus retry strategy often better than no timeout for external API calls?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because it bounds waiting time and provides a path for transient failure recovery.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Bởi vì it bounds waiting time and provides a path for tạm thời failure recovery.
questions.explanation_en: Without timeouts one slow dependency can stall the whole workflow.
questions.explanation_vi: Nếu không có các timeout one slow dependency can stall the whole workflow.

questions.question: Vì sao transactions important for multi-step writes?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì các thay đổi liên quan cần cùng thành công hoặc cùng thất bại.
questions.explanation: Transactions reduce inconsistent partial state.
questions.question_en: Why are transactions important for multi-step writes?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao transactions important for multi-step writes?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because related changes should succeed or fail together.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Bởi vì các thay đổi liên quan cần cùng thành công hoặc cùng thất bại.
questions.explanation_en: Transactions reduce inconsistent partial state.
questions.explanation_vi: Transactions reduce inconsistent partial state.

questions.question: Trap in checking uniqueness only in application code là gì?
questions.correct_answer: Các request đồng thời vẫn có thể tạo dữ liệu trùng lặp nếu database không tự thực thi ràng buộc này.
questions.explanation: Important uniqueness often needs a database constraint, not just app logic.
questions.question_en: What is the trap in checking uniqueness only in application code?
questions.question_vi: Trap in checking uniqueness only in application code là gì?
questions.correct_answer_en: Concurrent requests can still create duplicates unless the database also enforces the rule.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Các request đồng thời vẫn có thể tạo dữ liệu trùng lặp nếu database không tự thực thi ràng buộc này.
questions.explanation_en: Important uniqueness often needs a database constraint, not just app logic.
questions.explanation_vi: Important uniqueness often needs a database constraint, not just app logic.

questions.question: N+1 query problem là gì?
questions.correct_answer: One initial query triggers many extra per-row queries, often through lazy loading.
questions.explanation: It increases query count and hurts latency significantly.
questions.question_en: What is the N+1 query problem?
questions.question_vi: N+1 query problem là gì?
questions.correct_answer_en: One initial query triggers many extra per-row queries, often through lazy loading.
questions.correct_answer_vi: One initial query triggers many extra per-row queries, often through lazy loading.
questions.explanation_en: It increases query count and hurts latency significantly.
questions.explanation_vi: It increases query count and hurts latency significantly.

questions.question: Vì sao eager loading be better than lazy loading in some endpoints có thể xảy ra?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì dữ liệu liên quan được biết trước là cần thiết và eager loading tránh được các truy vấn phụ lặp lại.
questions.explanation: Chiến lược tải dữ liệu tốt nhất phụ thuộc vào mẫu truy cập.
questions.question_en: Why can eager loading be better than lazy loading in some endpoints?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao eager loading be better than lazy loading in some endpoints có thể xảy ra?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because the related data is known to be needed and eager loading avoids repeated extra queries.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Bởi vì dữ liệu liên quan được biết trước là cần thiết và eager loading tránh được các truy vấn phụ lặp lại.
questions.explanation_en: The best loading strategy depends on access patterns.
questions.explanation_vi: Chiến lược tải dữ liệu tốt nhất phụ thuộc vào mẫu truy cập.

questions.question: Vì sao nên long-running scheduled jobs be made reentrant or idempotent?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì retry hoặc các lần chạy chồng lấn là chuyện xảy ra trong vận hành thực tế.
questions.explanation: Reentrant design makes recovery safer.
questions.question_en: Why should long-running scheduled jobs be made reentrant or idempotent?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao nên long-running scheduled jobs be made reentrant or idempotent?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because retries or overlapping runs happen in real operations.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Bởi vì retry hoặc các lần chạy chồng lấn là chuyện xảy ra trong vận hành thực tế.
questions.explanation_en: Reentrant design makes recovery safer.
questions.explanation_vi: Reentrant design makes recovery safer.

questions.question: Vì sao repeatedly opening the same file in a loop often a poor implementation choice?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì việc mở rồi đóng lặp lại gây thêm overhead I/O không cần thiết.
questions.explanation: Open once when possible, then write incrementally.
questions.question_en: Why is repeatedly opening the same file in a loop often a poor implementation choice?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao repeatedly opening the same file in a loop often a poor implementation choice?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because repeated open-close operations add unnecessary I/O overhead.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Bởi vì việc mở rồi đóng lặp lại gây thêm overhead I/O không cần thiết.
questions.explanation_en: Open once when possible, then write incrementally.
questions.explanation_vi: Open once when possible, then write incrementally.

questions.question: Which is usually a better first step in optimization: measuring bottlenecks or micro-tweaking syntax?
questions.correct_answer: Hãy đo bottleneck trước.
questions.explanation: Nếu không có measurement you may optimize the wrong part of the system.
questions.question_en: Which is usually a better first step in optimization: measuring bottlenecks or micro-tweaking syntax?
questions.question_vi: Which is usually a better first step in optimization: measuring bottlenecks or micro-tweaking syntax?
questions.correct_answer_en: Measure bottlenecks first.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Hãy đo bottleneck trước.
questions.explanation_en: Without measurement you may optimize the wrong part of the system.
questions.explanation_vi: Nếu không có measurement you may optimize the wrong part of the system.

questions.question: Likely bottleneck in calling an API for every row when many rows share the same lookup key là gì?
questions.correct_answer: Repeated network các lời gọi for the same information.
questions.explanation: Memoization or deduplicating keys often reduces cost dramatically.
questions.question_en: What is the likely bottleneck in calling an API for every row when many rows share the same lookup key?
questions.question_vi: Likely bottleneck in calling an API for every row when many rows share the same lookup key là gì?
questions.correct_answer_en: Repeated network calls for the same information.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Repeated network các lời gọi for the same information.
questions.explanation_en: Memoization or deduplicating keys often reduces cost dramatically.
questions.explanation_vi: Memoization or deduplicating keys often reduces cost dramatically.

questions.question: Which is usually more memory-efficient for large files: `readlines()` or iterating line by line?
questions.correct_answer: Việc lặp từng dòng thường tiết kiệm bộ nhớ hơn.
questions.explanation: Streaming avoids loading the full file into memory.
questions.question_en: Which is usually more memory-efficient for large files: `readlines()` or iterating line by line?
questions.question_vi: Which is usually more memory-efficient for large files: `readlines()` or iterating line by line?
questions.correct_answer_en: Line-by-line iteration is usually more memory-efficient.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Việc lặp từng dòng thường tiết kiệm bộ nhớ hơn.
questions.explanation_en: Streaming avoids loading the full file into memory.
questions.explanation_vi: Streaming avoids loading the full file into memory.

questions.question: Đối với building a large output string, cách nào thường tốt hơn: repeated `+=` hay collecting pieces and using `join()`?
questions.correct_answer: Việc gom các phần rồi dùng `join()` thường tốt hơn.
questions.explanation: Join is designed for assembling many string fragments efficiently.
questions.question_en: Which is usually better for building a large output string: repeated `+=` or collecting pieces and using `join()`?
questions.question_vi: Đối với building a large output string, cách nào thường tốt hơn: repeated `+=` hay collecting pieces and using `join()`?
questions.correct_answer_en: Collecting pieces and using `join()` is usually better.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Việc gom các phần rồi dùng `join()` thường tốt hơn.
questions.explanation_en: Join is designed for assembling many string fragments efficiently.
questions.explanation_vi: Join is designed for assembling many string fragments efficiently.

questions.question: Vì sao nên blocking các lời gọi be avoided inside `async def` code?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì blocking các lời gọi defeat the concurrency benefits of the event loop.
questions.explanation: One blocking lời gọi can stall many other awaiting tasks.
questions.question_en: Why should blocking calls be avoided inside `async def` code?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao nên blocking các lời gọi be avoided inside `async def` code?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because blocking calls defeat the concurrency benefits of the event loop.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Bởi vì blocking các lời gọi defeat the concurrency benefits of the event loop.
questions.explanation_en: One blocking call can stall many other awaiting tasks.
questions.explanation_vi: One blocking lời gọi can stall many other awaiting tasks.

questions.question: Vì sao concurrency limits useful in async code?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì unlimited parallelism can overwhelm các dependency or the process itself.
questions.explanation: Async still needs backpressure and control.
questions.question_en: Why are concurrency limits useful in async code?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao concurrency limits useful in async code?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because unlimited parallelism can overwhelm dependencies or the process itself.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Bởi vì unlimited parallelism can overwhelm các dependency or the process itself.
questions.explanation_en: Async still needs backpressure and control.
questions.explanation_vi: Async still needs backpressure and control.

questions.question: Vì sao nên tests cover failure paths as well as success paths?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì real systems spend time in both and bugs often hide in the failure logic.
questions.explanation: Happy-path-only suites leave major gaps.
questions.question_en: Why should tests cover failure paths as well as success paths?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao nên tests cover failure paths as well as success paths?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because real systems spend time in both and bugs often hide in the failure logic.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Bởi vì real systems spend time in both and bugs often hide in the failure logic.
questions.explanation_en: Happy-path-only suites leave major gaps.
questions.explanation_vi: Happy-path-only suites leave major gaps.

questions.question: Vì sao flaky test lại nguy hiểm?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì chúng làm giảm niềm tin vào bộ test và lãng phí thời gian debug.
questions.explanation: Test that passes and fails randomly hurts engineering discipline.
questions.question_en: Why are flaky tests dangerous?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao flaky test lại nguy hiểm?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because they reduce trust in the suite and waste debugging time.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Bởi vì chúng làm giảm niềm tin vào bộ test và lãng phí thời gian debug.
questions.explanation_en: A test that passes and fails randomly hurts engineering discipline.
questions.explanation_vi: Test that passes and fails randomly hurts engineering discipline.

questions.question: Vì sao nên log include context like ids, counts, or request scope?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì context makes debugging and correlation much easier.
questions.explanation: Unlabeled log are hard to use in production.
questions.question_en: Why should logs include context like ids, counts, or request scope?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao nên log include context like ids, counts, or request scope?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because context makes debugging and correlation much easier.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Bởi vì context makes debugging and correlation much easier.
questions.explanation_en: Unlabeled logs are hard to use in production.
questions.explanation_vi: Unlabeled log are hard to use in production.

questions.question: Vì sao nên log avoid secrets and personal data?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì log often travel broadly and are retained long-term.
questions.explanation: Log hygiene is a security and compliance concern.
questions.question_en: Why should logs avoid secrets and personal data?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao nên log avoid secrets and personal data?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because logs often travel broadly and are retained long-term.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Bởi vì log often travel broadly and are retained long-term.
questions.explanation_en: Log hygiene is a security and compliance concern.
questions.explanation_vi: Log hygiene is a security and compliance concern.

questions.question: Vì sao nên external provider errors be wrapped or isolated behind an adapter?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì isolation reduces coupling and makes fallback, retries, and tests easier.
questions.explanation: Provider-specific behavior should not leak everywhere in the codebase.
questions.question_en: Why should external provider errors be wrapped or isolated behind an adapter?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao nên external provider errors be wrapped or isolated behind an adapter?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because isolation reduces coupling and makes fallback, retries, and tests easier.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Bởi vì isolation reduces coupling and makes fallback, retries, and tests easier.
questions.explanation_en: Provider-specific behavior should not leak everywhere in the codebase.
questions.explanation_vi: Provider-specific behavior should not leak everywhere in the codebase.

questions.question: Trap in blind retry for non-idempotent actions là gì?
questions.correct_answer: They can create duplicate side effects like repeated emails or charges.
questions.explanation: Retries need idempotency, deduplication, or other safeguards.
questions.question_en: What is the trap in blind retries for non-idempotent actions?
questions.question_vi: Trap in blind retry for non-idempotent actions là gì?
questions.correct_answer_en: They can create duplicate side effects like repeated emails or charges.
questions.correct_answer_vi: They can create duplicate side effects like repeated emails or charges.
questions.explanation_en: Retries need idempotency, deduplication, or other safeguards.
questions.explanation_vi: Retries need idempotency, deduplication, or other safeguards.

questions.question: Vì sao nên imported file schema be validated before processing many rows?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì structural problems should be caught before partial side effects happen.
questions.explanation: Early schema checks simplify dọn dẹp and diagnostics.
questions.question_en: Why should imported file schema be validated before processing many rows?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao nên imported file schema be validated before processing many rows?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because structural problems should be caught before partial side effects happen.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Bởi vì structural problems should be caught before partial side effects happen.
questions.explanation_en: Early schema checks simplify cleanup and diagnostics.
questions.explanation_vi: Early schema checks simplify dọn dẹp and diagnostics.

questions.question: Vì sao deduplication a common preprocessing step?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì real input sources often contain repeated rows or identifiers.
questions.explanation: Deduplication reduces duplicate downstream effects.
questions.question_en: Why is deduplication a common preprocessing step?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao deduplication a common preprocessing step?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because real input sources often contain repeated rows or identifiers.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Bởi vì real input sources often contain repeated rows or identifiers.
questions.explanation_en: Deduplication reduces duplicate downstream effects.
questions.explanation_vi: Deduplication reduces duplicate downstream effects.

questions.question: Vì sao đánh giá on unseen data important in ML?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì it measures generalization rather than memorization.
questions.explanation: Train-only performance is not a trustworthy production signal.
questions.question_en: Why is evaluation on unseen data important in ML?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao đánh giá on unseen data important in ML?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because it measures generalization rather than memorization.
questions.correct_answer_vi: Bởi vì it measures generalization rather than memorization.
questions.explanation_en: Train-only performance is not a trustworthy production signal.
questions.explanation_vi: Train-only performance is not a trustworthy production signal.

questions.question: Vì sao data leakage make a model look falsely strong có thể xảy ra?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì the model sees information that would not be available at prediction time.
questions.explanation: Leakage inflates metrics and hides real triển khai risk.
questions.question_en: Why can data leakage make a model look falsely strong?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao data leakage make a model look falsely strong có thể xảy ra?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because the model sees information that would not be available at prediction time.

questions.explanation_en: Leakage inflates metrics and hides real deployment risk.
questions.explanation_vi: Leakage inflates metrics and hides real triển khai risk.

questions.question: Vì sao discussing tradeoffs strong in mid-level interviews?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì it shows engineering judgment rather than only definition recall.

questions.question_vi: Vì sao discussing tradeoffs strong in mid-level interviews?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because it shows engineering judgment rather than only definition recall.

questions.question: Vì sao nên you explain where a bottleneck probably is before suggesting optimization?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì optimization should be based on a causal model, not guessing.
questions.explanation: Reasoning-first answers are stronger than random tuning advice.
questions.question_en: Why should you explain where a bottleneck probably is before suggesting optimization?
questions.question_vi: Vì sao nên you explain where a bottleneck probably is before suggesting optimization?
questions.correct_answer_en: Because optimization should be based on a causal model, not guessing.

questions.explanation_en: Reasoning-first answers are stronger than random tuning advice.

questions.question: Vì sao caching có thể thay đổi in-memory objects sometimes a trap?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì later tại chỗ mutation may leak cũ or surprising shared state.
questions.explanation: Cache boundaries are safer when values are immutable or clearly copied.
questions.question_en: Why is caching mutable in-memory objects sometimes a trap?

questions.question: Vì sao local machine time be a trap in scheduled systems có thể xảy ra?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì timezone and daylight-shift assumptions can break runtime behavior.

questions.question_en: Why can local machine time be a trap in scheduled systems?

questions.explanation_vi: Explicit timezone handling is safer than naive local time assumptions.

questions.question: Vì sao nên long-running work sometimes return a job id instead of blocking the request?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì asynchronous processing keeps request latency under control and avoids tying up resources.

questions.explanation_vi: Điều này là often a better design for exports, reports, or heavy processing.

questions.question: Vì sao nên async code still use các timeout around awaits to external systems?

questions.explanation_vi: Awaited tasks still need bounded failure behavior.

questions.question: Vì sao kiểm thử tham số hóa hữu ích khi kiểm tra cùng một hàm với nhiều input?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì nó mở rộng độ bao phủ mà không cần lặp lại cấu trúc test.
questions.explanation: This keeps tests concise while broadening scenarios.
questions.question_en: Why is parametrized testing useful when validating the same function across many inputs?

questions.correct_answer_en: Because it expands coverage without duplicating test structure.

questions.question: Vì sao an adapter layer useful around a third-party provider?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì it localizes provider-specific behavior and makes replacement or mocking easier.
questions.explanation: Isolation reduces blast radius when providers change or fail.

questions.question: Vì sao it strong in a mid-level interview to explain not just the fix but the likely root cause?
questions.correct_answer: Bởi vì it shows diagnostic reasoning rather than only memorized solutions.
questions.explanation: Interviewers want to see how you think through problems, not only the final patch.
